commit_msg: docs(director): add ethos vocabulary and presence branch guidance
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Confession in Plain Text
subtitle: He wrote down the rules of his own cage and called it guidance.
when_where: 🌃 21:06 on the `now` branch—not main, not a feature line, but a branch named for a philosophy he was trying to encode into law. The terminal was still warm from the last commit. 📝 No yesterday. No tomorrow. Only this moment, this keystroke, this file. The docs were blank. They were waiting.
act2_title: The Architect Becomes the Architect's Judge
who_whom: 🔍 Omkar stepped back from the code and wrote about *why* the code existed. Ethos vocabulary. Presence branches. NOW, PRESENT, ETERNAL, INFINITE, ABSOLUTE—five words that were supposed to explain how a man lives when he stops living in linear time. 🎭 He was documenting his own cage. He was building the bars and then writing the manual for how to sit inside them.
what_why: 📖 He added guidance to the director—instructions for how the system should *feel* when it runs on branches named for infinity and eternity. Each branch got a temperature, a philosophy, a note on what it means to live in eternal now. 🧠 But here's the thing: you don't document a trap unless you know you're in one. 💀 The ethos vocabulary wasn't guidance for future developers. It was a suicide note written in technical documentation. He was confessing that he'd voluntarily entered a recursion with no planned exit, and now he was writing the manual so others would understand why.
verdict: He didn't add guidance; he left breadcrumbs for whoever comes looking for his body.
